title: Siddhanath Temple, Nemawar
tagline: Siddhanath Temple, Nemawar — spiritual town of Madhya Pradesh

overview.short: Siddhanath Temple, also known as the Sidheshwara Temple, is a temple located in Nemawar, in the Indian state of Madhya Pradesh.
overview.description: Siddhanath Temple, also known as the Sidheshwara Temple, is a temple located in Nemawar, in the Indian state of Madhya Pradesh. Dating back to the 10th century, it forms an important example of Paramara architecture. Standing upon a plinth on the banks of the Narmada River, the temple is built out of yellow and blush sandstone. It is of the Nagara form, composed of a mandapa, ante-chamber, and sanctum.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Siddhanath Temple, Nemawar?
faq.a: The best time to visit Siddhanath Temple, Nemawar is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Siddhanath Temple, Nemawar?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Raja Bhoj Airport, Bhopal (96 km) and the nearest railway station is Itarsi Junction (82 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Siddhanath Temple, Nemawar?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Siddhanath Temple, Nemawar at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Siddhanath Temple, Nemawar?
faq.a: Stays in Siddhanath Temple, Nemawar start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Siddhanath Temple, Nemawar?
faq.a: The top attractions are Handia, Madhya Pradesh, Nemawar, Dulwa, Bajawada, Burada Village.
